ships.notes: Class labeled 'Adventure Class' on official fact sheet (ex-P&O Australia Pacific Encounter). Fact sheet at /news/ path.

exp.overview: A former P&O Australia ship (built 2002) now sailing for Carnival in the South Pacific, with the EDGE adventure park and multiple pools.

neighbourhoods.name: Oasis
neighbourhoods.desc: Top-deck relaxation area with pools and loungers.

neighbourhoods.name: Byron Beach Club
neighbourhoods.desc: Exclusive beach-club-style retreat for suite guests (extra).

neighbourhoods.name: Cloud 9 Spa
neighbourhoods.desc: The ship's spa neighborhood with thermal suites, treatment rooms and spa staterooms.

dining.name: Waterfront
dining.desc: The ship's main restaurant, serving sit-down, multi-course dinners with a nightly rotating menu.

dining.name: Angelo's
dining.desc: Italian restaurant that doubles as the exclusive suite-guest breakfast venue.

dining.name: Dragon Lady
dining.desc: Pan-Asian restaurant serving wok dishes and Asian classics.

dining.name: The Pantry
dining.desc: Casual all-day buffet with international stations, carvery, deli, salads and desserts.

dining.name: Fahrenheit 555 Steakhouse
dining.desc: Carnival's upscale steakhouse serving premium steaks, seafood and chops in a fine-dining setting (cover charge).

dining.name: Bonsai Sushi Express
dining.desc: Grab-and-go sushi counter for rolls, bowls and Japanese small plates.

dining.name: Trattoria
dining.desc: Casual Italian restaurant of pasta and regional favorites.

dining.name: New Zealand Natural
dining.desc: Ice-cream and dessert counter.

activities.name: four pools with waterslides
activities.desc: Top-deck aqua park with racing and spiraling waterslides and splash-zone fun for all ages.

activities.name: EDGE (ziplining, rock climbing, abseiling; extra charge)
activities.desc: Top-deck adventure park with ziplining, rock climbing and abseiling (extra charge).

activities.name: Dive-In Movies
activities.desc: Poolside big-screen movie showings on the Lido deck, day and night.

activities.name: video arcade (extra charge)
activities.desc: Arcade with video and redemption games (extra).

activities.name: Cloud 9 Spa / day spa & salon (extra charge)
activities.desc: Full-service spa with thermal suites, saunas, treatment rooms and salon (treatments extra).

activities.name: Byron Beach Club (extra charge)
activities.desc: Exclusive beach-club-style retreat for suite guests, with premium loungers and service (extra).

entertainment.name: The Punchliner Comedy Club
entertainment.desc: Punchliner Comedy Club with family-friendly early shows and adult late-night sets.

entertainment.name: stage shows
entertainment.desc: Live entertainment including production shows, live music and comedy.

entertainment.name: deck parties
entertainment.desc: Deck parties and a range of bars and lounges around the ship.

exp.kids_family: Camp Ocean for ages 2-11 (Penguins 2-5, Sharks 6-8, Stingrays 9-11), Circle “C” for tweens 12-14, and Club O2 for teens 15-17, plus Night Owls late-night care.
exp.who_for: Australian families and groups after a big-ship South Pacific holiday from local ports.

needs_manual: features: Official fact sheets list no named onboard venues/features for any ship; per-ship features left empty. Named features would need to be read from each carnival.com/cruise-ships/<ship> page if desired.
flag: All 29 in-service ships have official specs (name/class/year/tonnage/guests) from Carnival newsroom fact sheets. Roster confirmed against carnival.com/cruise-ships; excluded not-yet-in-service Carnival Tropicale (2028) and Carnival Festivale (2027). Class labels are Carnival's official fact-sheet wording (e.g. 'Venice Class' for Firenze/Venezia, 'Adventure Class' for Adventure/Encounter, 'Splendor Class', 'Sunshine Class'). Carnival Sunrise year (1999) is the vessel's original service entry per its official fact sheet; renamed 2019.
